texts.full_text: I have to make entities serializable when using EJB3? I use all of my entities Serializable. However I think it is okay to use without serializable. But what are the advantages of making an entity Serializable? When do we really have to serialize an entity / or when do not? A: From the Java EE 6 Tutorial (whicHere's a possible completion based on your input: From the Java EE 6 Tutorial, which states that "All entities can be serializable if they are used in any of the following contexts:" 1. `@Stateful` or other container-managed beans 2. JMS (Java Message Services) 3. Java TimerService This means you don't really try to get all your entities Serializable, but not only those that use these specific features. Also, there are benefits to creating an entity Serializable: * It requires the persistence context to be serialized and deserialized. * This is possible for `@Stateful` beans where a new instance can be created from the persisted state when it's injected into another

The act of breastfeeding creates a special bond between mother and child as it requires the mother to embrace her child several times each day. The babies, in turn, find comfort in their mothers' arms and learn to trust that they have a safe place to go for love and Sustenance. In the period of two years, something special has undoubtedly taken place between mother and child.It is rare these days to find women who breastfeed their children for two whole years as recommended in Islam. Attitudes have changed, and many women have been convinced that powdered formula (a manufactured drink!) is Better than their own milk! May all women reflect upon Allah's greatness and have confidence in their ability to produce milk in order to nourish their children in the best possible manner. Do not ever forget that formula is a 20th-century invention and that babies since the beginning of time have thrived without it. texts.full_text: nd from various angles. It's a testament to both our curiosity and our human nature to seek understanding beyond what we know or can see. When someone believes they have found "the ultimate question," whether it pertains to life, the universe, morality, existence itself—it often becomes their guiding light. However, asking this same profound question repeatedly doesn't mean you're stuck in a rut; rather, each iteration might reveal new insights as your understanding and perspective evolve. It's about exploring different dimensions of that singular yet complex query, much like peeling back layers to find the core essence of something deeply meaningful or enigmatic. In conversations with others who share this pursuit—or those who challenge it—you're forced into a continuous process of learning and re-evaluating your stance on what you thought was already clear. This is where disagreement can be healthy; it's an opportunity for growth, reflection, and perhaps even discovering that the ultimate question isn't singular but multifaceted. The act of questioning

texts.full_text: of the NCs $f_{v}=V_{NC}/V_{SC}$ where $V_{NC}$ and $V_{SC}$ are the volumes of the NC and the (computational) supercell, respectively. Throughout this work, for the sake of generality the nonlinear properties of the embedded NC systems are calculated at the unity filling factor which is indicated by an overbar $$\begin{aligned} \label{chi3} \overline{\chi}^{(3)}_{dcba}(-\omega_{3};\omega_{\gamma},\omega_{\beta},\omega_{\alpha})&\equiv &\frac{\chi^{(3)}_{dcba}(-\omega_{3};\omega_{\gamma},\omega_{\beta},\omega_{\alpha})}{f_{v}}\end{aligned}$$ where the subscripts $\{a,b,c,d\}$ refer to cartesian indices, $\omega_{\gamma}$, $\omega_{\beta}$, and $\omega_{\alpha}$ are the input frequencies, $\omega_{3}\equiv\omega_{\gamma}+\omega_{\beta}+\omega_{\alpha}$. Our results can trivially be converted to any specific filling factor. As we consider only spherical NCs, we set the cartesian tensor indices as $\{a,b,c,d\}$=$\{1,1,1,1\}$ and suppress these subscripts for convenience. Furthermore, we use a Lorentzian energy broadening parameter of 100 meV at full width. The value refers to the typical room temperature photoluminescence (hence, interband) linewidth of a *single* Si NC embedded in SiO$_2$ as considered in this work [@valenta02]. This parameter not only broadens the resonances but also introduces the band tailing effects [@lloyd] which becomes especially important in the transparency regions. At high illumination intensities, third-order changes in the refractive index and the absorption are observed due to the virtual and real excitations of the bound charges. Accounting for these effects, the refractive index and the absorption become, respectively, $n=n_{0}+n_{2}I$ and $\alpha=\alpha_{0}+\beta I$, where $n_{0}$

texts.full_text: tage Site, the Historical Monuments of Ancient Nara. These include the ancient imperial palace, a Shinto shrine, five Buddhist temples, and the associated grounds. Onetemple is Todai-ji which was constructed during Emperor Shotoku’s reign (724 to 748 A.D.) It houses one of Japan largest bronze statues: Buddha Vairocana at over fifty feet tall. The original statue dates from around 752; however it has been repaired twice, once after an earthquake in the twelfth century and again following a fire caused by lightning strike. The current building housing this massive work was constructed between 1705-9 but is still only two-thirds of its predecessor which burned down during civil unrest. The grounds are extensive with many other buildings including another temple containing three more large statues, all from about the same time period as Todai-Ji’s Buddha. texts.full_text: nter, and their enantiomers often exhibit different biological activities. Therefore, the separation of these enantiomers is crucial for pharmaceutical and clinical analyses. The separation buffer consisted of a phosphate buffer at a specific pH and concentration, with the addition of vancomycin at varying concentrations to optimize the enantioselectivity. The electrophoretic mobility of the quinolonecarboxylic acid enantiomers was measured as a function of vancomycin concentration. The changes in mobility and enantioselectivity were attributed to the formation of diastereomeric complexes between the quinolonecarboxylic acid enantiomers and vancomycin. Several experimental parameters were investigated to achieve the best separation, including the pH of the buffer, the concentration of vancomycin, the applied voltage, and the capillary temperature. The pH of the buffer was found to significantly influence the enantioselectivity, as it affects the ionization state of both the quinolonecarboxylic acids and vancomycin. The concentration of vancomycin was also critical, as it determined the extent of interaction with the analytes. The results showed that vancomycin was effective as a chiral selector for the enantioselective separation of quinolonecarboxylic acids. The enantiomers of various quinolonecarboxylic acids, such as ofloxacin, norfloxacin, and ciprofloxacin, were successfully separated with good resolution and enantioselectivity. The optimized conditions for each quinolonecarboxylic acid were determined, and the method was validated for linearity, precision, and accuracy. In conclusion, the use of vancomycin as a chiral selector in CZE provides a robust and efficient method for the enantioselective separation of quinolonecarboxylic acids. This method can be applied in pharmaceutical and clinical analyses to ensure the quality and safety of chiral drugs. Further studies may explore the applicability of this method to

texts.full_text: bioprosthetic valves. The frequency spectrum of the first heart sound (S1) was measured noninvasively in 54 patients with porcine bioprosthetic valves inserted in the mitral position. Phonocardiograms were recorded on magnetic tape on line with a signal processor with which the frequency spectrum and peak frequency of S1 were determined. In 19 patients with normal natural mitral valves, the apparent peak frequency within the range of measured frequencies of S1 was 46 +/- 2 Hz (mean +/- SEM). In 11 patients with porcine bioprosthetic valves implanted in the mitral position for less than or equal to 11/2 years, the apparent peak frequency of S1 was 43 +/- 3 Hz, which was not significantly different from S1 in patients with normal natural valves. However, in 33 patients with porcine bioprosthetic valves implanted in the mitral position for more than 11/2 years, the apparent peak frequency of S1 was 37 +/- 2 Hz, which was significantly lower than that in patients with normal valves or valves implanted for less than or equal to 11/2 years. These findings suggest that the peak frequency of the first heart sound can

texts.full_text: Continuation of the Text: Rather than confront these challenges with appropriate de-escalation strategies, the hospital staff resorted to restraints and seclusion as a standard response to the client’s outbursts. The psychiatric tech assigned to her care, who had not received adequate training in trauma-informed care or crisis management, frequently isolated the client in seclusion rooms for extended periods without proper supervision. During these times, the tech had unrestricted access to the client, despite her known vulnerability and history of being targeted for abuse in prior institutional settings. The hospital’s failure to maintain patient confidentiality further exacerbated the situation. The tech had accessed the client’s private medical records, including her history of sexual abuse and suicidal ideation, and used this information to manipulate and intimidate her. texts.full_text: drugs often display important side effects due to their systemic actions ([@B4]). Hence, there is urgent need for novel and safe therapeutic approaches to block and reverse the current obesity epidemic. Research has increasingly focused on understanding mechanisms underlying obesity development as well as identifying new targets that can be modulated with fewer adverse reactions. One promising area of research involves targeting gut microbiota through prebiotics, probiotics, or fecal microbial transplantation ([@B5]). These approaches aim to restore a healthy balance in the intestinal flora which has been shown to influence metabolism and energy homeostasis. Another emerging strategy is pharmacological modulation of brown adipose tissue (BAT) activity; enhancing BAT can increase thermogenesis leading to greater calorie expenditure without necessarily reducing food intake or increasing physical exertion ([@B6]). Additionally, there's growing interest in the role that circadian rhythms play in obesity and metabolic health—disruptions such as those caused by shift work have been linked with increased risk of weight gain. Interventions aimed at improving sleep quality and aligning eating patterns more closely to natural light cycles may offer non-invasive ways to combat this issue ([@B7]). texts.full_text: say. Duanmu Baiye narrowed his eyes at Han Yunxi, before suddenly dashing in front of her. But at the same time, Long Feiye also stepped forward and placed her behind him, giving Duanmu Baiye a cold stare to express his displeasure. Though she knew Long Feiye was protecting her for the reason of the Life Blood Pill, Han Yunxi still felt her heart warm up slightly. ThIs at least proved that she was worth the protection of the world’s foremost sovereign. “What are you two playing at?” Duanmu Baiye finally lost it. Although it had only been one day and one night, he was still distressed over his little sister. Yao Yao was the golden branch and jade leaf[2], doted on since youth in the palm of everyone’s hand. Even when she was training martial arts with her Master, she’d never suffered lIke this! Furthermore, Han Yunxi had set up this chessboard from the start, and now she was here watching the joke. Staying here any longer was just pure humiliation for both of them. This was the first time that Duanmu Baiye had been beaten by a woman, and he couldn’t take the shame! “Crown Prince Ye, don’t be impatient. I haven’t even found the third plant yet. If you can’t wait, you could always give up. I won’t stop you,” Han Yunxi reminded him sincerely. Long Feiye roughly pushed him aside until he crashed into a nearby wall. His reply was cold. “It has nothing to do with you. Either admit your loss or wait on the side.” Duanmu Baiye clenched his fists but could do nothing except keep waiting. Very quickly, half a day passed. Han Yunxi basked in the sun as she drank tea, occasionally sending glances to Duanmu Baiye with a smile that wasn’t a smile, provoking him immensely. Duanmu Baiye could only stew in his anger. He’d been proud and arrogant

texts.full_text: list of tips that will help you make the most of your freshman year. 1. Crack your door open You’ll find that some of the best friends you’ll make can be found on your floor or in your building. Make yourself accessible by leaving the door open, especially during common social times like evenings and weekends. 2. Get involved early Clubs are a great way to meet people who share similar interests with yours; they're also excellent opportunities for learning new skills outside of class time that can enrich your college experience in various ways—whether it's improving public speaking, honing leadership abilities or simply having fun while doing something you love. 3. Utilize resources Most colleges and universities offer a plethora of services designed to help students succeed: academic advisories for choosing the right courses; career centers with internship opportunities like Vocatio that Paula interned at over summer break—these are just two examples among many others available on campus or online, so take advantage! 4. Manage your time wisely Balancing schoolwork and social life is a challenge in college but an essential skill to develop early for future success. 5. Embrace change with open arms Your freshman year will be full of 'firsts': the first big test week; dining hall food that's finally starting not just taste like cardboard (at least, most days); your newfound freedom and responsibility... all these experiences are part what makes college unique—and they're worth embracing fully. 6. Stay connected with family While it’ll be tempting to dive headfirst into the independence of campus life after moving out for schooling away from home doesn't mean you have forgotten about those who love, support your most important relationships by maintaining regular communication and returning visits when possible—it can help keep a sense balance in all

We discuss the case of an adult male who presented acutely with a tender inguinal swelling and raised inflammatory markers and was therefore deemed as requiring surgical exploration. However, preoperative abdominal computerized tomography (CT) imaging revealed unexpected findings indicative of spontaneous thrombosis in his congenital extrahepatic portosystemic shunt (Abernethy malformation). This condition can cause symptoms that mimic inguinal hernia incarceration due to the increased portal pressure and subsequent venous congestion. texts.full_text: broadcast in the same way. There are shortwave radios, long wave radios, medium wave radios and even FM radio. So what exactly are the differences between them? What makes them unique or different from each other? In this article, we will limit ourselves to discussion between shortwave vs longwave radio, the two most commonly used devices. Table of Contents What Are Shortwave and Longwave Antenna and Quality Of Reception Shortwave vs Longwave Radios To Conclude What Are Shortwave and Longwave When we talk of shortwave and longwave, we are essentially referring to the range or frequency at which these radio waves operate. The terms 'shortwave' (SW) and 'longwave' (LW), as used in telecommunications engineering specifically, refer to two distinct bands within what is known broadly as the electromagnetic spectrum; they encompass specific segments of frequencies ranging from roughly 160 meters down through various sub-bands up until about one tenth that frequency. However briefly mentioned for a high school level radio enthusiasts can suffice: Shortwave broadcasts are characterized by their ability - sometimes even necessity due to atmospheric reasons on lower shortwaves bands—to reach far distances while generally carrying intelligible voice and music content at frequencies much higher than AM band or FM used in other more conventional forms of radiocommunication such as television, etc. They're essential for international broadcast media including national networks between continents during wartime or even recent times over private channels like BBC World Service amongst others! On the contrary where longer wavelength radio waves reaching much beyond Earth curvature and hence assuring reception in specific smaller regions within coverage area; Where interference from manmade electronic devices such as mobile phones, computers is significantly higher leading them to be regarded secondary modes of transmitting personal messages or local community based data. Hence technology supporting longwave transmissions essentially exists only for sending time signals used by predominantly synchronizing worldwide internet & GPS functionalities among others etc.. Add to library View PDF Abstract Background: Menopause can lead to alterations in women's health, with changes in the oxidative status of postmenopausal women in whom information regarding the influence of hormone therapy (HT) on antioxidant enzyme activities is limited. Objective: To evaluate the influence of HT on catalase activity; concentrations of lipids and lipoprotein, cholesteryl ester transfer protein, acid-reactive substances, nitrates, high-sensitivity C-reactive protein and carotid thickness in postmenopausal women. Methods: Ninety-four consecutive women were allocated to one of four groups, without HT and with HT. The latter group was subdivided into women using and those using estrogen plus progestogen thThe groups were: 1. without HT, 2. with estrogen-only HT, 3. with estrogen plus progestogen HT, and 4. with estrogen plus progestogen HT and statin. The study found that HT, particularly estrogen plus progestogen, significantly reduced catalase activity and attenuated cardiovascular risk. The HT groups, particularly estrogen plus progestogen, had significantly lower levels of lipids, lipoprotein, and thiobarbituric acid-reactive substances, and lower carotid thickness. The HT groups also had significantly lower levels of nitrates and high-sensitivity C-reactive protein. The addition of statin to HT had no significant effect on catalase activity, but further reduced levels of nitrates and high-sensitivity C-reactive protein. No kinematic difference was found between movements executed with and without visual control of both hand and targets. These results could be due to computation of the general program of an action that takes into account extrinsic properties of the final target. Conversely, they could depend on a visual interference effect produced by the near second target on the control of the first motor act. In order to dissociate the effects due to second target distancefrom those caused by spatial relations between targets and hand, experiment 2 was performed in which the subjects were required to reach-grasp an object placed either over or under two fixed targets (experiment 1). The kinematics of reaching did not differ when the first motor act was executed with respect to a single or double target. Conversely, kinematic differences emerged for movements towards both visible and invisible targets. Thus it is possible that the effects observed in experiment 1 were due to visual interference produced by the near second target. Titolo: Planning an action. IDEAL FSE addresses these issues by decomposing the signal into fat and water components, leading to improved image quality. No, the lost opportunities relate to education and appear in a few dimensions: student understandings, teacher practices and curriculum re-invention. Student Understandings: from the World Wide Web to the New WWW In the mid-90s, schools tended to be the place where most students first experienced the Internet. Given this blank slate, I had a goal: to make students’ first interactions with the Web powerful experiences in which they pursued meaningful topics, grappled with complex challenges, got feedback from real-world experts and created engaging ICT productions as part of their solutions. With a start like this, I hoped they would be forever disposed to the positive ways they could use technology. Part of this motivation for WebQuests came from a student in my ninth grade English class. As I tried (feebly) to engage this young man in an activity, all he could do was look up and say, “Ev”. Perplexed, I asked him to explain. “It is short for whatever.” Somehow, schoolwork evinced not only apathy, but some reduced fragment of it. One of my central beliefs is that the joy of learning is positive experience that unites humanity, so I tweaked WebQuests to maximise research into human motivation, aiming to orchestrate opportunities for students to sense the vitality that real learning sparks. However, within ten years I was compelled to write about ‘the new WWW’: the mobile-based, personally-targeted, immediately gratifying digital world that could provide people, especially children, with whatever they wanted, whenever and wherever. "We owe it to the traveling public to have only the most qualified and best trained pilots." According to the FAA, the new regulations stem in part from the crash of Colgan Air 3407 in February 2009. An investigation of the crash revealed that pilot Renslow, had failed three "check rides" (the flying equivalent of driver proficiency tests) and may not have had adequate training to respond to the emergency leading up to the crash. The FAA offers a variety of pilots licenses and certificates, each with a different set of privileges. These levels include Student, Recreational, Sport, Private, Commercial And Airline Transport Pilot. Pilots with a student pilot certification are not permitted to fly solo and are barred from carrying passengers. Sport pilot certificate holders can not carry more than one passenger and are permitted to only fly light-sport aircraft during the daytime.
